name: gcp
description: Inspect Google Cloud Compute Engine instances, Cloud Run services, and Cloud Functions, and start or stop VM instances

tools.name: List Compute Instances
tools.description: List Compute Engine VM instances in a specific zone of a project

filter.description: Filter expression, e.g. status = "RUNNING" or name = "my-instance-*"

maxResults.description: Maximum instances to return per page

pageToken.description: Continuation token from a previous page (nextPageToken)

tools.name: List All Compute Instances
tools.description: List Compute Engine VM instances across all zones of a project (aggregated list), useful when you don't know which zones are in use

maxResults.description: Maximum instances to return per page

pageToken.description: Continuation token from a previous page (nextPageToken)

returnPartialSuccess.description: Return partial results instead of failing when some zones are unreachable

tools.name: Get Compute Instance
tools.description: Get one Compute Engine VM instance's details - machine type, status, IPs, and disks - e.g. to confirm a start or stop completed

zone.description: Compute Engine zone of the instance, e.g. us-central1-a

instance.description: Name of the VM instance to describe

tools.name: List Zones
tools.description: List the Compute Engine zones available in a project, so agents can discover valid zone names for the zone-scoped tools

filter.description: Filter expression, e.g. name = "us-central1-*" or status = "UP"

maxResults.description: Maximum zones to return per page

pageToken.description: Continuation token from a previous page (nextPageToken)

tools.name: Start Compute Instance
tools.description: Start a stopped Compute Engine VM instance (returns a zone operation that completes asynchronously) — requires the full cloud-platform scope

zone.description: Compute Engine zone of the instance, e.g. us-central1-a

instance.description: Name of the VM instance to start

tools.name: Stop Compute Instance
tools.description: Stop a running Compute Engine VM instance so it stops incurring compute charges (returns a zone operation that completes asynchronously) — requires the full cloud-platform scope

zone.description: Compute Engine zone of the instance, e.g. us-central1-a

instance.description: Name of the VM instance to stop

tools.name: List Cloud Run Services
tools.description: List Cloud Run services in a project location (region)

location.description: Cloud Run region, e.g. us-central1 (must be a valid region; Cloud Run does not accept the '-' wildcard)

pageSize.description: Maximum services to return per page

pageToken.description: Continuation token from a previous services.list call (nextPageToken)

tools.name: Get Cloud Run Service
tools.description: Get one Cloud Run service's details - URL, container image, environment, and traffic split

location.description: Cloud Run region of the service, e.g. us-central1

service.description: Name of the Cloud Run service to describe

tools.name: List Cloud Functions
tools.description: List Cloud Functions (2nd gen API) in a project location — requires the optional cloud-platform scope (the only scope the Cloud Functions API accepts)

location.description: Function location, e.g. us-central1, or '-' to list functions across all locations

pageSize.description: Maximum functions to return per page (up to 1000)

pageToken.description: Continuation token from a previous page (nextPageToken)

orderBy.description: Comma-separated list of fields to sort by

prompts.title: Compute inventory
prompts.prompt: List my Compute Engine instances across all zones and summarize their machine types and power states.

prompts.title: Serverless overview
prompts.prompt: List my Cloud Run services and Cloud Functions in a region and summarize what is deployed.

prompts.title: Stop an idle VM
prompts.prompt: Find running Compute Engine instances and help me stop one that looks idle so it stops incurring charges.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: Google Cloud setup

steps.title: Create or select a Google Cloud project
steps.description: Go to https://console.cloud.google.com and create a project (new Google Cloud accounts include free-tier credit, and Compute Engine offers an always-free e2-micro instance in some US regions for testing).

steps.title: Enable the required APIs
steps.description: Enable the Compute Engine API (https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/library/compute.googleapis.com), the Cloud Run Admin API (https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/library/run.googleapis.com), and the Cloud Functions API (https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/library/cloudfunctions.googleapis.com) for your project.

steps.title: Create OAuth credentials
steps.description: In https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/credentials create an OAuth 2.0 Client ID (Web application) and add your callback URL as an authorized redirect URI. Store the values as GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID and GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET.

steps.title: Create test resources
steps.description: Create a small VM instance (e2-micro) in Compute Engine and deploy a sample Cloud Run service so the list tools have something to return. Stop the VM when done to avoid charges.

notes: The default scopes are read-only (compute.readonly and run.readonly) and cover listing Compute Engine instances and Cloud Run services; listing Cloud Functions and starting or stopping VM instances require granting the optional cloud-platform scope during authorization.
The signed-in user also needs IAM permissions on the project: roles/viewer covers the list tools, and roles/compute.instanceAdmin.v1 (or broader) is needed to start and stop instances.
Start and stop return a zone operation immediately; the instance state changes asynchronously, so re-run List Compute Instances to confirm.

Use '-' as the location to list Cloud Functions across all locations in one call (unreachable regions are reported in the 'unreachable' field); Cloud Run does not support the '-' wildcard, so list its services one region at a time.
The Cloud Functions v2 API also returns 1st-gen functions, with the generation indicated by the 'environment' field.
